title: peltopus ortalis
side_effects: Peltopus ortalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate, nausea, and dizziness. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to stomach pain, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can cause liver damage and kidney failure in severe cases. Additionally, improper use of the plant can trigger allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and swelling.
